Computer Studies.q: The early mechanical counting device consisting of a wooden frame with parallel rods and sliding beads, invented by the Chinese, is the ________.
Which historical mathematical device, consisting of rods carved from bones, was invented by John Napier to perform multiplication and division calculations?

The first mechanical adding machine capable of performing addition and subtraction directly using a complex system of interlocking gears and wheels was invented by Blaise Pascal and named the ________.
Who is globally recognized as the 'Father of the Modern Computer' due to his design blueprints for the Difference and Analytical Engines?
The prominent electronic components that served as the primary switching circuits inside First-Generation computers like the ENIAC were ________.
Second-Generation computers replaced bulky vacuum tubes with a much smaller, faster, and more energy-efficient component called a/an ________.
The defining electronic breakthrough that characterized Third-Generation computing frameworks from 1964 to 1971 was the ________.
Fourth-Generation computers, which are widely used today, utilize which electronic component to house millions of transistors on a single silicon chip?
Fifth-Generation computing research and development frameworks focus primarily on integrating which technology into machines?
A type of computer designed specifically to process continuous physical measurements, such as temperature, pressure, and voltage fluctuations, is a/an ________.
Computers that process information strictly in discrete, binary forms using digits 0 and 1 are classified as ________.
The largest, most powerful, and exceptionally expensive class of computers used by global weather agencies and nuclear research centers to process complex calculations is a/an ________.
An example of a microcomputer designed to be compact enough to fit flat on top of a standard office desk space is a ________.
Any physical part of a computer system that you can touch, see, and handle with your hands is collectively termed computer ________.
The electronic data processing system relies on an operational loop. What does the acronym 'GIGO' stand for in computing?
Which of the following computer hardware devices functions strictly as an input module used to feed raw text alphanumeric data into the system unit?
A handheld pointing device equipped with a tracking sensor and buttons, used to navigate and click items on a graphical interface, is the ________.
Which computer input device acts like an electronic photocopier by converting printed physical paper documents or photos into digital image files?
An input device commonly used by video gamers to steer aircraft or control characters inside simulation gaming software layouts is a ________.
The primary computer output hardware device designed to display processing results visually as soft-copy images or text screens is the ________.
An output hardware device used to translate digital files from a computer into hard-copy paper prints using ink or toner powders is a ________.
Which type of heavy-duty printer is specifically engineered to print large-scale architectural blueprints, engineering designs, and massive maps?
The main electronic circuit board inside the computer system unit housing the CPU, RAM sockets, and expansion slots is the ________.
The central processing unit (CPU) is often described structurally as the ________ of the computer system.
Which specific component of the Central Processing Unit (CPU) executes all mathematical calculations and logical comparisons?
The internal component of the Central Processing Unit (CPU) responsible for directing and coordinating data signals across all hardware units is the ________.
The volatile internal primary memory of a computer that stores active files and programs currently being processed by the CPU is ________.
Why is a computer's Random Access Memory (RAM) classified strictly as a volatile storage medium?
o: It cannot hold large alphanumeric text files
It loses all its stored data instantly when power supply is turned off
It can only be read from but never written to
It is built entirely from physical moving glass disks
It is permanently welded inside the processor chip

Computer Studies.q: The non-volatile primary memory chip containing pre-recorded, permanent startup instructions (BIOS) written by factory manufacturers is ________.
The smallest unit of measuring digital data inside a computer system, representing a single binary state of 0 or 1, is a ________.
A cluster of exactly ________ consecutive bits forms one single Byte of data storage.
How many individual bits are contained within a single data unit structurally known as a 'Nibble'?
The primary high-capacity internal magnetic secondary storage device housed inside the system unit to store files permanently is the ________.
The collection of electronic instructions, programs, and data configurations that tell computer hardware exactly what to do and how to do it is computer ________.
Computer software is broadly divided into two major functional categories: ________ software and ________ software.
The core background system software program that acts as an intermediary layer between hardware circuits and users, managing all files, is the ________.
Which of the following computer software items represents a popular and modern desktop graphical operating system platform?
An example of a specialized utility software program engineered to scan, detect, quarantine, and eliminate malicious viruses from computer storage is a/an ________.
Software programs created explicitly to help users perform distinct tasks like typing office letters, playing music tracks, or drawing shapes are ________.
Which application software program is best suited for designing promotional flyers, typing essays, and formatting official letters?
An application software package organized in rows and columns, designed primarily to handle complex accounting balances and mathematical charts, is a ________.
The entire systematic administrative process of switching on a computer system from a completely powered-off state until it loads up the desktop screen is ________.
What specific type of booting occurs when a user powers on a computer that was completely turned off by pressing its physical power button?
A 'Warm Boot' refers to the process of resetting or restarting an active computer system that is already running, which can be triggered using the hotkeys ________.
The basic keyboard operation or shortcut combination utilized to safely 'Copy' a highlighted text block or file into memory is ________.
Which keyboard shortcut combination is pressed down to paste an originally copied file or text segment into a new location?
The fundamental keyboard shortcut combination used to save an ongoing file project inside most application software windows is ________.
A cluster or collection of two or more independent computer machines linked together electronically using cables or wireless channels to share files is a/an ________.
Which type of computer network configuration covers a small, localized geographical area like a single office room, house, or school building?
The vast, public worldwide interconnection of computer networks spanning across continents to share data globally is the ________.
An electronic software application program used exclusively to access, open, and navigate web page contents on the World Wide Web is a/an ________.
Which of the following websites serves as a powerful search engine platform designed to hunt down and index public information based on keywords?
The electronic communication service that allows internet users to compose, send, transmit, and receive digital mail letters over networks instantly is ________.
In a formal email address profile like `student@laseb.gov.ng`, what functional component does the text segment `@` represent?
o: The 'At' symbol separating the username from the domain name

Computer Studies.q: The malicious, harmful software program intentionally written by cybercriminals to damage files and disrupt computer operations is a computer ________.
A fraudulent practice where cybercriminals send deceptive emails mimicking legitimate banks to trick users into revealing secret passwords or PIN details is ________.
An electronic or software security barrier setup engineered to monitor incoming and outgoing network traffic to protect systems against hackers is a ________.
The technical practice of breaking up a computational task across multiple physical microprocessors working simultaneously to accelerate processing speed is ________.
Any computer hardware layout configuration that can handle user touch inputs natively on its screen surface without keyboards is a ________.
The personal computer components, human operators, and designers who build and command computational workflows are classified as ________.
